seoTitle: Record Owner Contributions in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Log personal funds invested into your business correctly in QuickBooks Online using a bank deposit mapped to an equity account, not income.
intro: Injecting your own money into the business to cover costs needs to be recorded as equity, not income, or it will artificially inflate your tax liability. This guide walks through recording an owner's contribution correctly using QuickBooks Online's bank deposit tool.

steps.heading: Open the Bank Deposit form
steps.body: Click the + New button, then choose Bank Deposit under the Other column.

steps.heading: Select the receiving account
steps.body: In the Account dropdown, choose the specific business checking account where the funds were physically deposited.

steps.heading: Map the funds to an equity account
steps.body: Under Received From, select your owner profile, then choose your dedicated Owner's Contribution or Partner's Capital account from the Account column, not an income category.

steps.heading: Enter the amount and payment method
steps.body: Key in the precise dollar value, choose the payment method, and add a clear note in the memo box.

steps.heading: Save and verify the bank match
steps.body: Click Save and Close, then check Bank Transactions to confirm the live bank feed line pairs correctly against your recorded deposit.

faq.question: Why shouldn't I record an owner's contribution as income?
faq.answer: Mapping it to an income category artificially inflates your tax liabilities since it isn't actually revenue from business operations.

faq.question: What account type should owner contributions be mapped to?
faq.answer: They should be mapped to an Equity account, such as Owner's Contribution or Capital Investment, in your Chart of Accounts.

faq.question: How do I confirm the deposit matches my bank feed?
faq.answer: Check the Bank Transactions section to see the downloaded bank line automatically pair with your manually recorded deposit entry.

faq.question: Which reports show my recorded owner contributions?
faq.answer: The Statement of Cash Flows and Balance Sheet reports both reflect your injected equity once it posts.
